status_detail: wrtn chat API mechanism fully identified. Chat uses Socket.IO WebSocket, NOT standard HTTP POST. Warning text NOT rendered (avatar only).

results.critical_finding: Chat message POST is NOT visible in DevTools Network. NOT caught by fetch/XHR/EventSource JS interceptors. wrtn sends chat via Socket.IO WebSocket bidirectional channel.
investigation_steps: 1. Sent 'hello test message' - wrtn responded normally via Socket.IO
2. Filtered Network by api.wrtn.ai - only GET requests visible (metadata, agent, messages)
3. Searched 'test api discovery' in Network Search - No matches found
4. Installed fetch() interceptor - only caught tracking POSTs (Facebook pixel, mpc2-prod)
5. Installed XMLHttpRequest interceptor - only caught mixpanel/clarity tracking
6. Installed EventSource interceptor - no EventSource creation detected
8. Found wrtn.socket.io WebSocket connection (status 101) in Network tab

warning_test.warning_detail: 서포터 avatar (fox icon) appears but NO text rendered. Same as #476.
warning_test.websocket_error: WebSocket connection to wss://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn.socket.io/ failed: invalid
warning_test.console_errors: 4 errors total after keyword message (up from 2 before)

notes: wrtn uses Next.js frontend with Socket.IO for chat. The chat message and streaming response both flow through WebSocket, bypassing standard HTTP request/response. Etap's APF must intercept at WebSocket data frame level. The path_patterns='/' config may need adjustment since the actual WebSocket endpoint is /wrtn.socket.io/. The 'openai_compat_sse' envelope (id=34) configured for wrtn may not correctly handle Socket.IO protocol framing.
